section_title: INTRODUCTION TO E-MINI S&P FUTURES & DAILY CHART OVERVIEW
content:
Good evening folks. We are taking a look at the E-mini S&P Futures Contract for June 2023, specifically the
daily chart.
I want to cover some of the points I'll be looking for tomorrow in terms of key levels and areas of interest.
- Here is the premium fair value gaps, outside imbalance, buy side inefficiency or SIBI.
- Consequent encroachment premium Wick High midpoint level is noted.
- The imbalance here, which is a SIBI, essentially is equilibrium between this high and that low, noted in a
light gray area.
- Discount encroachment of this Wick, midpoint of that, is what's annotated.
- The Fair Value Gap over here, because we cut through candles, we're not supplying demand. This level is
still going to be useful going forward, just as well as this one is as well.

Dropping down into the hourly chart here, you can see the buy side liquidity pool, an area of interest for
tomorrow.
- We have a breaker here: a low, high, lower low.
- Chaining off of that old imbalance on the daily chart, the discount fair value gap is present.
- The breaker is extended through.
- We have a small fair value gap in the form of a SIBI (sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency).
- Price is down to consequent encroachment and also the breaker there, prior to rallying up and taking out
short-term relative equal highs, which is buy side liquidity.
- Market trades back down into the high of the Fair Value Gap here and basically just consolidates into the
close for today.
- These levels will be of interest going forward into tomorrow.
15
- MINUTE TIME FRAME:

In the 15-minute time frame, we had a buy side liquidity pool here, as I mentioned, relative equal highs. The
market traded above that and then once more here. We talked about that live on Twitter.
- I was looking at 4034 buy side.
- Inside of that run, from the high, low, higher high, down closed candle here
- that's your bearish breaker.
Extend that forward.
- We will roll it back into the morning session right after the 9:30 opening.
- Note this discount Wick consequent encroachment beautiful delivery there.
- The Fair Value Gap that was on the hourly chart doesn't look so much like an imbalance on the 15-minute
time frame, but we work top-down.
- We can see how we touched that bullish breaker here again.
- Go back through the video, screenshot your charts from the video playback and transpose that to your
own charts, and then you will see what it is that I'm showing you here. It'll be much more meaningful should
you do it on your charts, not just looking at mine, especially by watching tomorrow's live price action.
5
- MINUTE CHART:

section_title: MIDNIGHT OPENING PRICE AND PRICE DELIVERY
content:
On the five-minute chart here, the opening price right here at midnight that's this level here, so New York
midnight opening price.
- We ran above buy side here, and I mentioned we would be looking for 4034 buy side, and it rallied up into
that here.
- All of this is due to swing, we're moving above the New York midnight opening candle opening price.
- Rise up buy side, then we're going to be looking for 4018 sell side, which is below this low here.
- All of this back and forth price action finally gave up the ghost and traded back down into old order flow in
the form of that discount Wick consequent encroachment.
- We're going to dig into a little bit more detail.
- Again, you can see the breaker extended throughout the day to the last hour of trading, which was
influential once more there.
1
- MINUTE CHART:

section_title: EARLY SESSION BUY SIDE AND SELL SIDE TARGETS
content:
Getting down to the brass tacks, we have a one-minute chart here.
- There is an imbalance here; extend that through again because we're not supplying demand; we cut
through candles. That range I projected forward and showed that on Twitter today as well.
- We talked about how 4034 buy side would be in contention and a volume imbalance. I shared this on
Twitter as well right in here. We were looking for 4034 buy side, so a really nice run up into that for a five
- handle run.
- It stopped dead in its tracks at the macro at 9:50, which is the high candle here, to 10:10. So that's our
first macro after the opening bell at 9:30.
- Algorithmic delivery takes it right into buy side, and then at that time, we took our attention to 4018 sell
side.
- The market trades back down, works inside that old imbalance we cut through candles, and there's
another volume imbalance right here. Again, draw that out in time.
- It hits it as resistance, now trades softer. Another imbalance I mentioned this real-time in Twitter as well.
- Body to the candles more or less respects the majority of that range, and then we had this one more little
return back up into the original imbalance I mentioned here, inside of that bearish breaker.
- All of this may be confusing if you're just casually watching and you're not really trying to see where these
levels are derived from and not transposing them on your own charts. I promise you, you're wasting your
time. You have to do the work of taking the information and placing it on your charts and then cycling
through top-down. Top-down meaning from daily chart to hourly chart to 15-minute time frame to five
- minute to now one-minute.
- The market sells off here, attacks the sell side there for another five handles. So we had a five-handle run
there and a five-handle run there, so that's ten handles that I mentioned live on Twitter as it delivered, and
then I said, 'Okay, I'm done. I'm going to take my attention away from the charts.'

section_title: OPTIMAL TRADE ENTRY AND OPENING RANGE GAP LOW
content:
It trades back up into the imbalance here, so that would be an optimal trade entry and also a return back
into a Fair Value Gap.
- Market sells off, institutional order flow entry drill inside this area here. It doesn't fill in that gap in other
words, the market aggressively trades lower.
- We have this wick in here, okay, so it's a discount wick before trading down to the opening range gap low.
And I mentioned this on Twitter as well; I showed you the chart how to derive that information.
- Then it comes back up, hits it beautifully. That's algorithmic price delivery.
- It sells off once more and goes down into the opening range gap low and then ultimately later on, trades
down into that old discount Wick consequent encroachment, which is right here.
- And the current New Week Opening Gap High, New Week Opening Gap consequent encroachment, and
New Week Opening Gap low, shown here respectively.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING THE OPENING RANGE GAP AND ALGORITHMIC MACROS
content:
Here is the opening range gap shown to you at the 9:30 opening price.
- With regular trading hours, you'll see that down here. If you have live data, you can toggle this; it'll be
ETH, which is electronic trading hours, or regular trading hours, which is the RTH here.
- When you toggle that, it's going to show you where we closed the previous session, right here. And then
we wait for the 9:30 opening; there's your opening range gap.
- You can see how we performed there. We had an initial drop down. That drop was followed by the run up
into the buy side I mentioned real-time. We hit that right at the candle high at 9:50.
- That's the algorithmic macro that I teach, which is 10 minutes to 10 and it continues to 10 minutes after
10. Within that 10 minutes prior and 10 minutes after the 10 o'clock hour, there will be a price swing that
originates and starts there, and it'll run for liquidity.
- We take buy side here, so where's it going to go next? Sell side. And I called that live all on Twitter, and
it's all there. You're welcome to go back and look at all that. I didn't do too many tweets today, so it'll be
easy to track it and see it.

section_title: ICT BACKTESTING METHODOLOGY AND INVERSION LEVELS
content:
The market trades again back up into that Fair Value Gap I mentioned here.
- You can take the tweets also and copy the link from Twitter. In a right mouse button on a TradingView
chart, on a one-minute chart, it'll plot my tweet real-time right on the minute candle. You can see what I was
talking about at the time in proper context. It's a very useful way of following along the logic, even after the
fact, like if you have to go to school, run a business, or working or sleeping. You can go back and do all
these backtesting ideas here.
- My personal backtesting is all of this annotation. It's not pushing a button with a Market Replay type thing,
nothing like that. That's a forward walk test before you would do a live trading with real-time and also demo.
So there's a step between that and that; that's not what we're talking about here.
- This is backtesting, my version of it, which is studying the price action, looking at all the scenarios and
mapping out the idea with all this empty space here and here and over here. You'd be filling that in with
your own chart explaining what this return back to this consequent encroachment of that discount wick now
acting as what? An inversion level, resistance. Beautiful delivery.
- Now, this is where I tell the skeptics that watch this, and you see a rectangle on my chart, and they think
right away it's supply and demand. It's not. For the folks that say it's support and resistance, it's not. This
low, why did price not go just to that level right there and stop and turn on a dime if it's support broken turn
resistance? Why did it go up to the level I teach right there? Because the market's run by algorithmic price
delivery, not retail logic like support and resistance, okay?
- The market then trades into the Opening Range Gap Low. Beautiful delivery there, and then consolidates
going into the lunch hour.

section_title: LUNCH HOUR ACTION, OPENING RANGE GAP DETAILS, AND FIBONACCI
content:
PROJECTIONS
Speaking of lunch, here we have it. The market trades again off that consequent encroachment of that wick
down into the Opening Range Gap Low. And for your notice, the Opening Range Gap Low, when the
market opens higher than where we closed on the previous session (meaning where we settle and where
we open at 9:30 the next day), that is your Opening Range Gap. It may be a gap higher or it may be a gap
lower. In this case, we gap higher, so the Opening Range Gap Low is the previous day's settlement price or
close, okay?
- And then the difference is where we open at 9:30. If the gap's higher when they open at 9:30 from the
previous day's settlement price, chances are we're probably going to look to see it trade back down into
that range. It can go all the way down to the Opening Range Gap Low or beyond it if there's something else
that you could reach for for a discount.
- I've shown you this before: we have a low, lower low, higher low. This price run here, take your FIB, drop it
on the low up the high. It projects a swing high of negative 1.5, which comes to the price level of 4027 and
three quarters.
- Extend that to the right. 4027 and three quarters is the actual high tick of that candle, that candle, and that
candle in the last hour of trading, inside of that first Fair Value Gap I mentioned. That's the rules folks, that's
how it's coded, and I know it doesn't sound fun when I keep repeating it, but at some point, you're going to
have to realize that these things are not random.

section_title: POST-LUNCH MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT AND CONFLUENCE
content:
Part of that run up in here, we have a shift in market structure after one o'clock. Here's one o'clock, so now
what are we going to do? We're going to run for the stops prior to what, during, and prior to lunch.
- So we have all these buy side here, buy side here, and all the way back up above here.
- So we're in consolidation right now in the daily chart; that's the reason why we're seeing these types of
price action.
- Fair Value Gap high to low discount; trade into it. Look at the bodies respecting it. Yes, we have a wick and
we have a wick there that just about closed it in, but the bodies are respecting the high end of that. That's
beautiful.
- Price runs, takes out buy side, digs into the imbalance. Now, using this price run here to measure that
projection, from here to here, taking that buy side here, that suggested that that's a potential run.
- But if we go back to the old high back here early in the day at 4034, down to that low, it would be better for
us to dig up into a premium because we're in consolidation: buy side, buy side, buy side, and also with this
swing high here and the old Fair Value Gap. So there's a lot of confluence pointing to that 4027.75 level,
which it delivers perfectly to the tick three candles prior to it giving up the ghost.
- Also, note that that run up here is above the New York Midnight Opening Price. Okay, so you have to
know what time it is in New York local time. Right at midnight New York local time, that price you want to
have that annotated on your chart.
- Best short positions are going to be above it; best long positions are going to be below it. That rule will
serve you extremely well in the future.

section_title: FINAL HOUR ANALYSIS AND ALGORITHMIC DELIVERY
content:
Finally, this is the last hour of trading for the New York session, or final hour New York PM session. Here's
that 4027.75 level and the macro that starts at 2:52 to 3:10. That means there's going to be a move that
begins there.
- It's up in a premium; it's going to seek what? Discount.
- Here's your price low in that run. Equilibrium would be right in here, so right below that low would be nice.
- Interestingly enough, it trades down into that and retraces back up into the bearish breaker. This breaker
is the one I showed you at the beginning of the video, and we're extending it obviously across the entirety
of the trading day.
- So we have this break lower, swing lows taken, trades back up into this candle right here. That candle's
high is the exact high of that one and that one. That is institutional order flow entry drill. All it has to do is
match that price, so you could be a seller at that candle and that candle, looking for a price run below that
low here and that low over here (relative equal lows here). That's where sell side resides.
- And again, we have the Opening Range Gap Low over here, which is previous settlement price on Friday.
- And then we have again repricing to that inside the last portion of the final hour of PM session trading.
Let's go back into the details here, trading back up in that bearish breaker with also institutional order flow
entry drill after hitting the 4027.75 level perfectly. That's a nice short here.

section_title: ADVANCED ORDER FLOW CONCEPTS AND 2022 MODEL APPLICATION
content:
Volume imbalance, if you missed it, trades back up into it there.
- Breaks lower, we have a wick. This is a discount wick that's going to act as an inversion level, so think of
like support broken turn resistance, the real way.
- Then we have SIBI here (sell side imbalance buy side inefficiency), which is a Fair Value Gap, and I'm
classifying it as what it is: a SIBI. SIBI sell side delivery, what's it inefficient on? Buy side. So we would
expect price to deliver up to it, and look at that, wouldn't you know it, it goes right to the tick price. Don't
take my word for it, measure that. Go in your time frame and measure the low of that candle to the open
midpoint of that 50, draw that out in time, that's exactly where it goes.
- Inside of a bearish breaker, we have buy side here. We have a high, low, higher high. That last down
closed candle, extend it forward. We have a SIBI and a consequent encroachment on that wick. Boom,
delivery perfect.
- Price breaks lower. Last up close candle prior this displacement is a bearish order block. Trades up into
that once, twice, gives up the ghost and again one more time repricing back down into the Opening Range
Gap Low for a final hour New York session trading opportunity. Just a beautiful PM session, this one here is
like a centerfold.
- Also, notice again we're trading above, right here, about that New York Midnight Opening Price. Short of
the breaker, shorter the breaker, shorter the volume imbalance. Breaks lower below this low here, so
there's displacement. So now you can use what? You can use the 2022 model, which would be high to low
equilibrium. Here's your Fair Value Gap, trades up into it there. What you're going to aim for? Sell side,
relative equal lows, and or the Opening Range Gap Low.
- And then boom, just wonderful technicals, just really, really nice to see algorithmic theory delivering as
you see it here. Hopefully, you found this insightful. Until I'll talk to you next time, be safe.
